NAME: GeneBayes
FULL NAME: Bayesian estimation of gene constraint from an evolutionary model with gene features
KEYWORDS: gene constraint, Bayesian inference, selection coefficient, loss-of-function, s_het
USE: GeneBayes estimates gene-level selective constraint (s_het) by combining an evolutionary population genetics model with machine learning on gene features, improving constraint inference for short genes.
DESCRIPTION: Zeng, T., Spence, J. P., Mostafavi, H., & Pritchard, J. K. (2024). Bayesian estimation of gene constraint from an evolutionary model with gene features. Nature Genetics, 56, 1632-1643.
TITLE: Bayesian estimation of gene constraint from an evolutionary model with gene features.
ABSTRACT: This study introduces GeneBayes, a framework that integrates an evolutionary model with gene features to estimate gene-level selective constraint. The method improves inference of the interpretable constraint metric s_het, especially for short genes, and outperforms existing metrics for prioritizing genes relevant to essentiality and human disease.
CITATION: Zeng T, Spence JP, Mostafavi H, Pritchard JK. (2024) Bayesian estimation of gene constraint from an evolutionary model with gene features. Nature Genetics, 56, 1632-1643. doi:10.1038/s41588-024-01820-9.
